title: Method for diagnosing risk for inflammatory disease through glycan profiling
abstract: Methods of detecting altered HDL functionality as well as adjusting HDL functionality are described.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of determining high density lipoprotein (HDL) functional capacity in a human, the method comprising: a. obtaining an HDL sample from a human; b. detecting in the sample one or more level of glycosylation in the HDL sample; and c. comparing the level of the glycosylation to a control value, thereby determining HDL functional capacity in the human. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the detecting comprises detecting levels of glycosylation of one or more specific proteins in the HDL sample. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the detecting comprises detecting a level of at least one glycosylation type for all proteins in the HDL sample. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the control value is: a value indicating the level of glycosylation detected in a healthy individual; or a value indicating the level of glycosylation detected in an individual having dysfunctional HDL. 5. The method of claim 1 or 4, wherein the detecting comprises detecting the level of glycosylation of: ApoC-III, Mono-sialylated ApoC-III, ApoC-III_74_2221, Non-sialylated alpha-2HS -glycoprotein (A2HSG), Di-sialylated A2HSG, A2HSG_O_346_2200, A2HSG_176_5402, alpha- 1 -antitrypsin (Al AT), A1AT_70_5402, A1AT_271_5402, 

 A1AT_70_5412, A1AT_107_5412, or A1AT_271_5412. 6. The method of claim any of claims 1-5, wherein the detecting comprises detecting the level of glycosylation of: i. ApoC-III, and mono-sialic ApoC-III, and ApoC-III_74_2221 ; or ii. A1AT, and di-sialic A1AT (e.g., glycans 5402 and 5412); or iii. non-sialic A2HSG, and A2HSG_O_346_2200, and di-sialic A2HSG, and A2HSG_176_5402. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the HDL functional capacity is the level of pro-inflammatory HDL or HDL lacking in anti-inflammatory capacity in the sample. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising: d. administering an effective amount of dietary monosaccharides and/or altering dietary pattern to the diagnosed patient. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the dietary monosaccharides comprise at least one of the following in an amount sufficient to alter the level of glycosylation for at least one of the specific proteins in the human: galactose, fructose, fucose, or N-acetyl glucosamine. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, wherein the detecting comprise binding the specific protein in the sample with a binding reagent that specifically binds the specific protein or an oligosaccharide thereon. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the binding agent is a lectin or an antibody. 12. The method of any of claims 1-9, wherein the detecting comprises mass spectrometry or liquid chromatography. 13. A method of improving high density lipoprotein (HDL) function in a human in need thereof, the method comprising, administering a sufficient amount of monosaccharides to improve HDL function in the human, wherein before the administrating the human has HDLs having levels of glycosylation of one or more specific proteins indicative of reduced HDL function compared to healthy humans and wherein after the administrating the human has HDLs having levels of glycosylation of HDL proteins indicative of improved HDL function compared to before the administering. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the monosaccharides comprise at least or more of galactose, fructose, fucose, or N-acetyl glucosamine. 15. The method of claim 13 or 14, wherein before the administering, an HDL sample is obtained from the human and the level of glycosylation of at least one or more of the specific proteins is detected. 16. The method of any of claim 13-15, wherein after the administering, an HDL sample is obtained from the human and the level of glycosylation of at least one or more of the specific proteins is detected. 17. A reaction mixture comprising: a human sample enriched for high density lipoproteins (HDLs); and one or more reagent specific for a specific HDL protein or glycan in the sample. 18. The reaction mixture of claim 17, wherein the specific HDL protein is selected from ApoC-III, Mono-sialylated ApoC-III, ApoC-III_74_2221, Non-sialylated alpha-2HS -glycoprotein (A2HSG), Di-sialylated A2HSG, 

 A2HSG_O_346_2200, A2HSG_176_5402, alpha- 1 -antitrypsin (A1AT), A1AT_70_5402, A1AT_271_5402, A1AT_70_5412, A1AT_107_5412, or A1AT_271_5412. 19. The reaction mixture of claim 17, comprising: i. a reagent that specifically binds ApoC-III, and a reagent that specifically binds mono-sialic ApoC-III, and a reagent that specifically binds ApoC-III_74_2221; or ii. a reagent that specifically binds Al AT, and a reagent that specifically binds di-sialic Al AT (e.g., glycans 5402 and 5412); iii. a reagent that specifically binds non-sialic A2HSG, and a reagent that specifically binds A2HSG_O_346_2200, and a reagent that specifically binds di-sialic A2HSG, and a reagent that specifically binds A2HSG_176_5402.
